name: Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Twilio 24/7 for Standups

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf08470-task`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Read Metrics from Google Sheets

### Read Metrics from Google Sheets

Reads every row from 'Task Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Severity Signals · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Read Metrics from Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Compute Severity Signals

Custom JavaScript: Score each task 0-100 from simple heuristics

Next: Grade Severity Tier

### Grade Severity Tier

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: info, warning, critical + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Just Record (Info) · ②→ Warn Channel (Twilio) · ③→ Page Urgently (Slack) · ④→ Archive Unscored

### Just Record (Info)

Appends a new row to 'Task Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Warn Channel (Twilio)

Sends an SMS via Twilio — reserved for urgent alerts since SMS costs money per message, unlike chat notifications.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Twilio SID + token

### Page Urgently (Slack)

Posts a message to Slack (`#projects`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Archive Unscored

Appends a new row to 'Task Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Tier Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Tier Outcomes

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Stamp Processing Time

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#projects`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#projects`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Twilio 24/7 for Standups

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Broadcast stakeholders on changes with Twilio 24/7 for Standups. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual standups is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Read Metrics from Google Sheets → Compute Severity Signals → Grade Severity Tier → Just Record (Info) → Merge Tier Outcomes → Stamp Processing Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating standups in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Responds in seconds
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2; Twilio SID + token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Three response tiers, not a single yes/no alert, match the response to the severity — logging every metric quietly would bury the one critical spike, and paging on-call for every info-level blip trains them to ignore alerts entirely. Critical gets a distinct, louder channel from warning on purpose.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Adjust the tier thresholds and see how alert sensitivity changes.
2. Trigger a critical-tier value and confirm it pages a different channel than warning does.
3. Add a 4th tier between warning and critical.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your standups process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
